2013-11-01.title: Kenya: Somali Militants Attacked
2013-11-01.abstract: The Kenyan military led an African Union operation against the Shabab in southern Somalia on Thursday in an effort to vanquish the group that claimed responsibility for an attack on a Kenyan mall.

2013-11-01.title: Mexico: Junk Food Tax Is Approved
2013-11-01.abstract: Aiming to curb unhealthy consumption habits, Mexico’s Congress on Thursday approved new taxes on sugary drinks and junk food.

2013-11-01.title: South Sudan: One-Sided Vote in Disputed District
2013-11-01.abstract: Residents of Abyei voted overwhelmingly in favor of joining South Sudan, according to results announced on Thursday, but the nonbinding referendum was not recognized by Sudan and South Sudan.

2013-11-01.title: International Court Postpones Kenyatta’s Trial
2013-11-01.abstract: The International Criminal Court on Thursday postponed the trial of President Uhuru Kenyatta of Kenya on crimes against humanity charges until February.

2013-11-01.title: Secular Turkish Government Permits Religious Symbol
2013-11-01.abstract: Four female lawmakers were welcomed into Parliament with Islamic head scarves, which for some was an unwelcome break with secular traditions and for others a sign of the nation’s maturing democracy.

2013-11-02.title: Tabloid Hacked Phone of Prince Harry’s Secretary, Jury Is Told
2013-11-02.abstract: A message seeking help on a term paper led to an article in The News of the World, with some details left out, the prosecution said.

2013-11-02.title: A Fiscal Scold, Merkel Softens Tone at Home
2013-11-02.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel of Germany is being pushed to accept policies that would sharply increase domestic spending, even as she shows few signs of easing austerity for the rest of Europe.

2013-11-02.title: Egyptian Channel Blocks Episode by TV Satirist
2013-11-02.abstract: The program, whose host is the popular satirist Bassem Youssef, has been closely watched to see how much criticism of the government would be permitted.

2013-11-02.title: Surge in Iraqi Violence Reunites Maliki and Obama
2013-11-02.abstract: Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki of Iraq pressed President Obama at the White House on Friday for help in fighting Qaeda terrorists in his country’s lawless west.

2013-11-02.title: How the Pakistani Taliban Became a Deadly Force
2013-11-02.abstract: Formally founded in 2007, a group traces its roots to cross-border radicalization spread by the Afghan Taliban and fighters from Al Qaeda.

2013-11-03.title: Srdja Popovic, 76, Serbian Champion of Rights, Dies
2013-11-03.abstract: Mr. Popovic was a Serbian lawyer and celebrated human rights advocate who pressed for reform and free speech for five decades.

2013-11-03.title: Gérard de Villiers, 83, French Spy Writer, Dies
2013-11-03.abstract: Mr. de Villiers was a French popular novelist whose long-running spy-thriller series, S.A.S., sold more than 100 million copies.

2013-11-03.title: Stick Figures and Stunted Growth as Warring Syria Goes Hungry
2013-11-03.abstract: Across Syria, a country that long prided itself on providing affordable food to its people, efforts to ensure basic sustenance appear to be failing, and millions are going hungry.

2013-11-03.title: Reeling From War, Afghan Society Ill-Equipped to Face Addiction
2013-11-03.abstract: Afghanistan’s western border towns with Iran have become veritable zombie villages in recent years — encampments where entire families are shambling addicts.

2013-11-03.title: That Other Big Afghan Crisis, the Growing Army of Addicts
2013-11-03.abstract: A new report underscores a growing crisis in the city of Herat: one in every five households contains at least one drug user.

2013-11-04.title: Lonely Advocate for Restraint in Egypt Tiptoes Around Talk of Compromise
2013-11-04.abstract: With the country on war footing and security officials rallying a fight against Islamists, Ziad Bahaa el-Din, a cabinet minister, is in a precarious place, trying to coax the government toward moderation.

2013-11-04.title: In Pakistan, Drone Strike Turns a Villain Into a Victim
2013-11-04.abstract: Pakistan’s political leaders have reacted with unusual vehemence since missiles fired by American drones killed the leader of the Pakistani Taliban, Hakimullah Mehsud, on Friday.

2013-11-04.title: To Shape Young Palestinians, Hamas Creates Its Own Textbooks
2013-11-04.abstract: The Hamas movement is deviating from the approved Palestinian Authority curriculum, using the new texts as part of a broader push to infuse the next generation with its militant ideology.

2013-11-04.title: Iran’s Top Leader and U.S. Counter Criticism of Talks
2013-11-04.abstract: Iran’s supreme leader rebuked his country’s hard-liners, and the chief American negotiator offered assurances to Israel in a television interview.

2013-11-04.title: European Borders Tested as Money Is Moved to Shield Wealth
2013-11-04.abstract: Customs agents at borders of European countries in economic crisis say they are increasingly seizing undeclared cash exceeding the €10,000 that each traveler is allowed to carry.

2013-11-05.title: With Cows in Mind, Swiss Farmers Wage Litter Battle
2013-11-05.abstract: A traditionally tidy nation is being alerted to the danger, sometimes fatal, that refuse poses to livestock.

2013-11-05.title: Ukraine: Workers Removing Parts of Chernobyl Plant
2013-11-05.abstract: Workers at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant in Ukraine have begun dismantling a ventilation stack that has filtered radioactive gases from the reactor that was destroyed in an accident in 1986.

2013-11-05.title: Few Eager to Talk Peace in Syria, but a Mediator Won’t Stop
2013-11-05.abstract: Lakhdar Brahimi’s latest mission — starting Syria peace talks — has proved to be among the most stubborn of the many conflicts he has mediated on behalf of the United Nations.

2013-11-05.title: Uighur Scholar in Ugly Confrontation With Security Agents
2013-11-05.abstract: Ilham Tohti, arguably China’s most vocal advocate for the rights of its Uighur minority, said security agents in Beijing rammed his car, threatened to kill his wife and children and told him to stop speaking to foreign reporters.

2013-11-05.title: Americans Jailed for Months in Qatar After Daughter’s Death
2013-11-05.abstract: Supporters described the case as a combination of flawed or nonexistent evidence, ethnic prejudice and extreme cultural misunderstandings.

2013-11-06.title: China: Envoy Sent to North Korea to Push for Talks
2013-11-06.abstract: China said Tuesday that it had sent its top envoy to North Korea this week to push for the resumption of talks on denuclearizing the Korean Peninsula.

2013-11-06.title: Explosions Near Communist Party Building in China
2013-11-06.abstract: A series of explosions outside a Communist Party building in a northern Chinese city comes at a time of heightened security concerns in China.

2013-11-06.title: U.S. Is Losing Advantage in Spying, Report Says
2013-11-06.abstract: A congressional panel charged that the United States intelligence agencies’ research-and-development efforts were disorganized and unfocused.

2013-11-06.title: Afghan Militant Group Faces Unusual Discontent
2013-11-06.abstract: The militant Haqqani network’s war against the Soviets more than two decades ago is still spoken of in reverential tones in the mountains of eastern Afghanistan, but support has turned to resentment in some corners.

2013-11-06.title: Argentina Finds a Dictatorship’s Secret Records
2013-11-06.abstract: The discovery of secret documents from the military dictatorship that led Argentina from 1976 to 1983 provides rare insight into the persecution of intellectual figures.

2013-11-07.title: Peacekeepers in Congo to Focus on Another Rebel Group
2013-11-07.abstract: After routing the Congolese M23 rebels, United Nations peacekeepers are expected to turn their attention to subduing a Hutu militia.

2013-11-07.title: On Holding Hands and Fake Marriage: Stories of Being Gay in Russia
2013-11-07.abstract: To better understand what it is like to identify as L.G.B.T. in a country that officially discourages openness, The Times asked readers to share their stories.

2013-11-07.title: Swiss Report Supports Theory Arafat Was Poisoned
2013-11-07.abstract: A forensics report said that taking into account analytical limitations, its findings “moderately support the proposition” that Yasir Arafat’s death was the consequence of polonium poisoning.

2013-11-07.title: Ruble Hopes to Join Ranks of the $ and €
2013-11-07.abstract: Russian officials want to give the ruble a symbol that would become as easily recognizable as those for the dollar and the euro.

2013-11-07.title: Next Door to War, There’s Money to Be Made
2013-11-07.abstract: A half-dozen military supply stores are a source of military vests, camouflage pants, knives and gas masks for a clientele flooding in from all over the Middle East.

2013-11-08.title: West and Iran Seen as Nearing a Nuclear Deal
2013-11-08.abstract: Even as the two sides tried to finalize the agreement on Thursday, fissures have widened between the United States and some of its principal allies over the potential pact.

2013-11-08.title: Under Strain, France Examines Its Safety Net
2013-11-08.abstract: The pervasive presence of government in French life, from workplace rules to health and education benefits, is now the subject of a great debate.

2013-11-08.title: Moments in the Toronto Mayor’s Scandal
2013-11-08.abstract: Earlier this year, Mayor Rob Ford denied smoking crack cocaine but now admits to drug use and remains adamant that he will remain in office.

2013-11-08.title: Italy: Nazi War Criminal Buried at Jail
2013-11-08.abstract: The body of Erich Priebke, a former SS captain, has been buried in an anonymous grave in an abandoned cemetery in an Italian prison, the newspaper La Repubblica said Thursday.

2013-11-08.title: Rejected Seat on U.N. Panel Is Considered by Jordan
2013-11-08.abstract: Arab states are said to be conducting talks about which country might replace Saudi Arabia in a nonpermanent Security Council role.

2013-11-09.title: Video: The Fate of Egypt’s Jon Stewart
2013-11-09.abstract: A popular satirist, Bassem Youssef, was taken off the air after he made jokes about Egypt’s military leaders, triggering a debate on the military’s commitment to democracy.

2013-11-09.title: Central African Republic: Violence Increases Hunger Risk, U.N. Warns
2013-11-09.abstract: The World Food Program of the United Nations said in a statement that 1.1 million people are at risk of hunger and that their plight could worsen.

2013-11-09.title: Venezuela: U.S. Reporter Detained
2013-11-09.abstract: The Miami Herald’s Jim Wyss was picked up Thursday near the country’s western border with Colombia, according to an article on the newspaper’s website.

2013-11-09.title: With Survey, Vatican Seeks Laity Comment on Family Issues
2013-11-09.abstract: In a questionnaire being distributed to bishops in advance of their synod next fall, Pope Francis is asking Catholics for their thoughts and opinions on contentious social issues.

2013-11-09.title: Right Wing’s Surge in Europe Has the Establishment Rattled
2013-11-09.abstract: All over Europe, established political forces are losing ground to grass-roots insurgents fired by resentment against a governing class many see as out of touch.

2013-11-10.title: Obama’s Portable Zone of Secrecy (Some Assembly Required)
2013-11-10.abstract: When the president travels and needs to handle sensitive information, he ducks into a security tent set up by aides to shield himself from secret cameras and listening devices.

2013-11-10.title: Aided by Army of ‘Vapers,’ E-Cigarette Industry Woos and Wins Europe
2013-11-10.abstract: Eluding restrictions, e-cigarette sellers outgunned not just the tobacco companies but also pharmaceutical companies that make competing products for people trying to stop smoking.

2013-11-10.title: Elderly Woman’s Killing Lays Bare Myanmar’s Religious Divisions
2013-11-10.abstract: The stabbing death of a helpless 94-year-old woman is a stark symbol of the breadth of anti-Muslim feelings in a Buddhist-majority country.

2013-11-10.title: Storied Party of Mandela Faces South Africa Unrest
2013-11-10.abstract: Twenty years after the African National Congress swept to power in apartheid’s wake, discontent with its leaders is creating an opening for a new political party.

2013-11-10.title: Venezuela Releases U.S. Journalist After 2 Days
2013-11-10.abstract: Venezuelan officials released Jim Wyss, a bureau chief for The Miami Herald, after he was detained Thursday near the Colombian border.

2013-11-11.title: No Quick Fixes for China’s Overwhelmed Organ Transplant System
2013-11-11.abstract: China has vowed to end its reliance on executions for transplant organs, but public distrust has slowed development of a donation system.

2013-11-11.title: A Brazilian Boom Town of ‘Eternal Beauty’ Faces Its Troubled Side
2013-11-11.abstract: Salvador has experienced a surge in violent crime, chaotic traffic, and the metamorphosis of once-elegant seaside districts into crime-ridden areas.

2013-11-11.title: Removing Fuel Rods Poses New Risks at Crippled Nuclear Plant in Japan
2013-11-11.abstract: A plan to move radioactive fuel to safer storage at the Fukushima Daiichi power plant is a critical step in a long decommissioning process that has already had serious setbacks.

2013-11-11.title: Storm Track Veers Away From Vietnam
2013-11-11.abstract: After plowing through the Philippines, Typhoon Haiyan took a right turn over the South China Sea on Sunday, avoiding the central coast of Vietnam.

2013-11-11.title: Danger Persists for Reporters in Pakistan, Despite Vow to Protect Them
2013-11-11.abstract: Five journalists have died so far this year in Pakistan, and by some accounts, the dangers of reporting in the nation have been intensifying.

2013-11-12.title: Saudi Arabia: MERS Virus Found in Patient’s Pet Camel
2013-11-12.abstract: The discovery of the virus that causes Middle East Respiratory Syndrome in a camel belonging to a recent human victim adds credence to the theory that the virus originates in bats but can reach humans through camels.

2013-11-12.title: Russia: Four Are Sentenced for Airport Attack
2013-11-12.abstract: A Moscow court on Monday sentenced three men to life in prison and a fourth man to 10 years for their role in the 2011 suicide bombing at the Domodedovo airport.

2013-11-12.title: Russia: Greenpeace Detainees Are Transferred
2013-11-12.abstract: Russia’s prison authorities began transferring 30 crew members of a Greenpeace International ship from Murmansk to St. Petersburg on Monday.

2013-11-12.title: In Challenge, Former Rebels in Libya Form Own Oil Company
2013-11-12.abstract: The rebels’ establishment of a regional oil company to sell crude from oil fields and port terminals they occupy was seen as a symbolic move, but it was another escalation of tensions.

2013-11-12.title: Lawyer for Ukraine Opposition Leader Is Questioned
2013-11-12.abstract: The chief defense lawyer for the imprisoned opposition leader Yulia V. Tymoshenko was questioned on suspicion that he had beaten his former wife.

2013-11-13.title: Czech Politician Faces Claims of Aiding Secret Police
2013-11-13.abstract: Andrej Babis, a billionaire who was a surprise kingmaker in recent elections, denies allegations that he was an agent for the reviled Czechoslovak secret police.

2013-11-13.title: Born in Protest, a Soccer Team Hailed by the People and the Government
2013-11-13.abstract: Mouloudia, founded nearly 100 years ago and Algeria’s most popular soccer team, was a force in the war for independence from France and is a powerful political tool.

2013-11-13.title: The Passion (and the Politics) of an Algerian Soccer Team
2013-11-13.abstract: Mouloudia Algiers, called the beating heart of the nation, is deeply tied to Algeria’s history and politics. The club is by far the most popular team in the country.

2013-11-13.title: Private Donors’ Funds Add Wild Card to War in Syria
2013-11-13.abstract: The flow of millions of dollars in private money to extremist fighters has added an unpredictable factor to the war, analysts say, exacerbating divisions in the opposition.

2013-11-13.title: New U.N. Brigade’s Aggressive Stance in Africa Brings Success, and Risks
2013-11-13.abstract: The Force Intervention Brigade routed rebels in the Democratic Republic of Congo, a departure from peacekeepers’ usually passive approach.

2013-11-14.title: Syrian Forces Recapture Damascus Suburb From Rebels
2013-11-14.abstract: State news media showed government troops entering Hujeira, the latest in a string of suburbs south of Damascus where the government has made inroads in recent days.

2013-11-14.title: State Dept. Calls Group in Nigeria Terrorists
2013-11-14.abstract: The State Department has designated Boko Haram, a homegrown Islamist movement in Nigeria, as a terrorist group.

2013-11-14.title: Car Mechanic Dreams Up a Tool to Ease Births
2013-11-14.abstract: An Argentine car mechanic adapted a method to retrieve a cork from a wine bottle to develop a device that could be used to save a baby stuck in the birth canal.

2013-11-14.title: Kerry and Biden Ask for Room to Reach a Nuclear Deal With Iran
2013-11-14.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry and Vice President Joseph R. Biden Jr. pressed senators to give the Obama administration breathing room to reach an accord with Iran.

2013-11-14.title: Iran and Western Powers Clash Over Why Nuclear Talks Failed
2013-11-14.abstract: The United States says Iran was unwilling to agree to a unified proposal, while Iran says France thwarted progress.

2013-11-15.title: Cameroon: French Priest Kidnapped
2013-11-15.abstract: There has been no claim of responsibility, but the Nigerian Islamist militant groups Boko Haram and Ansaru are known to operate in the region, near the border with Nigeria.

2013-11-15.title: In Venezuela, Campaign Takes the Form of a Fire Sale
2013-11-15.abstract: President Nicolás Maduro is trying to quell frustration with the economy ahead of municipal elections by forcing retailers to slash prices.

2013-11-15.title: Government in Egypt Eases Restrictions
2013-11-15.abstract: The interim government lifts a state of emergency and a nighttime curfew after three months — an indication of its confidence in its control of the country.

2013-11-15.title: On a Mission Within Earshot of a War, Armed With Paint
2013-11-15.abstract: A group of graffiti painters from several countries gathered in the disputed region to add color to a building’s ruins.

2013-11-15.title: U.S. Dancer Quits the Bolshoi, Complaining of Bribery
2013-11-15.abstract: Joy Womack, an American dancer at the prestigious Bolshoi Ballet, says she was told she would have to pay a $10,000 bribe to get a solo role.

2013-11-16.title: Beheading in Syria Is Called a Mistake
2013-11-16.abstract: Jihadists in Syria beheaded a half-conscious man believed to be an enemy combatant and a Shiite — only to find that he was a rebel fighter, too.

2013-11-16.title: To a Philosopher-General in Israel, Peace Is the Time to Prepare for War
2013-11-16.abstract: The departing commander of Israel’s Army in northern Galilee, Brig. Gen. Herzl Halevi, is convinced that Israel will go to war again with Lebanon.

2013-11-16.title: Libya Protest Turns Deadly as Militias Open Fire
2013-11-16.abstract: Dozens of people were killed in the Libyan capital, Tripoli, setting off some of the worst violence there since the revolt against Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi almost three years ago.

2013-11-16.title: African Call to Delay Kenyans’ Trials Fails at U.N.
2013-11-16.abstract: The International Criminal Court’s charges of crimes against humanity has led to tension with the African Union.

2013-11-16.title: Brazil: Arrests Begin in Vote-Fraud Case
2013-11-16.abstract: The federal police began arresting a dozen powerful political figures, bankers and businessmen convicted in a vast vote-buying scandal that surfaced more than eight years ago.

2013-11-17.title: Growing Clamor About Inequities of Climate Crisis
2013-11-17.abstract: The devastation in the Philippines has revived the debate over how to address the disproportionate effects of climate change on poorer countries, an issue that has gained momentum but faces daunting challenges.

2013-11-17.title: Relief Supplies Pour Into Philippines, but Remote Areas Still Suffer
2013-11-17.abstract: While hard-hit urban areas are finally getting adequate supplies, the rural hinterland has been largely left to fend for itself in the week since Typhoon Haiyan hit.

2013-11-17.title: Joaquín Hernández Galicia, Oil Workers Chief, Dies at 91
2013-11-17.abstract: Mr. Hernández Galicia led a union of 200,000 workers employed by Mexico’s huge state-owned oil company, Pemex, and they revered him for the generous benefits he won for them.

2013-11-17.title: Keith Bradsher Answers Readers’ Questions on the Philippines
2013-11-17.abstract: Mr. Bradsher, a Times reporter covering the aftermath of Typhoon Haiyan, answered readers’ questions about doing his job under difficult conditions and the relief efforts underway.

2013-11-17.title: Aleksandr Serebrov, 69, Dies; Cosmonaut Who Persevered
2013-11-17.abstract: Mr. Serebrov, who once held a spacewalk record, flew four missions for a program that abandoned Soviet ambitions for Russian spare parts.

2013-11-18.title: Afghan Talks at Impasse Before Vote, Officials Say
2013-11-18.abstract: Days before an Afghan grand council is scheduled to meet, Afghan officials said both sides had refused to budge on American insistence that United States troops retain the right to enter Afghan homes.

2013-11-18.title: West Faces Challenge in Moving Syrian Chemical Arms Through Battlefields
2013-11-18.abstract: A plan to move more than 600 tons of precursor chemicals out of Syria in vehicles is raising concerns that such a convoy would present a slow-moving target for Syrian opposition groups.

2013-11-18.title: Where St. Nicholas Has His Black Pete(s), Charges of Racism Follow
2013-11-18.abstract: In Dutch tradition, Peter, portrayed by people in blackface makeup, sports outlandish Renaissance costumes, with thick red lips and frizzy hairdo wigs or fake dreadlocks.

2013-11-18.title: Libyan Official Abducted Amid Unrest in Capital
2013-11-18.abstract: The City Council in Tripoli, where the deputy intelligence chief was seized, called a general strike to demand that militias leave the city.

2013-11-18.title: Bomb at Base Kills at Least 31 Syrian Troops
2013-11-18.abstract: The attack, one of the deadliest against government soldiers in recent months, came as an offensive against rebels continued on three fronts.

2013-11-19.title: Slowdown in Carbon Emissions Worldwide, but Coal Burning Continues to Grow
2013-11-19.abstract: Experts said the world remains far from meeting international goals on climate change.

2013-11-19.title: Kenya: New Details on Mall Attackers
2013-11-19.abstract: The attackers behind the siege of the Westgate shopping mall had been living in Nairobi and plotted the attack for several months, a Western official briefed on the investigation said.

2013-11-19.title: Venezuela: Former Bank Official Pleads Guilty in Bribery Case
2013-11-19.abstract: The executive, María de los Angeles González, 55, agreed to cooperate with prosecutors, suggesting there could be further revelations about corruption at the government-controlled bank, the Economic and Social Development Bank of Venezuela.

2013-11-19.title: Chile: Former President’s Coalition Kept in Check
2013-11-19.abstract: Former President Michelle Bachelet was unable to elect enough lawmakers from her coalition to pass sweeping political proposals, but she still may be able to push through many of her social and economic policies if she is elected.

2013-11-19.title: Egypt: Security Official Assassinated
2013-11-19.abstract: Gunmen killed a senior Egyptian security official whose responsibilities included investigating Muslim extremists, the Interior Ministry said Monday.

2013-11-20.title: A New Party Challenges the One That Has Run India for Most of Its History
2013-11-20.abstract: As elections approach in India, a new political party, Aam Aadmi, dedicated to the common man and to eliminating corruption, hopes to end the Congress Party’s long dominance.

2013-11-20.title: N.S.A. Spying Scandal Hurts Close Ties Between Australia and Indonesia
2013-11-20.abstract: Indonesia has taken offense at reports that its close trading partner, Australia, used its embassies in Asia to collect intelligence.

2013-11-20.title: Small Protest in Tahrir Square Restores Dissent to Cairo’s Heart
2013-11-20.abstract: The demonstration seemed to represent a breakthrough for young leftists and other revolutionary activists who have struggled to find their voice since the military ousted President Mohamed Morsi in July.

2013-11-20.title: Typhoon Response Highlights Weaknesses in Philippine Military
2013-11-20.abstract: Typhoon Haiyan has focused an unflattering spotlight on the Philippine military, an overstretched, poorly funded force that has been criticized for its late arrival to the disaster zone.

2013-11-20.title: Toronto Residents Have Funny Feeling About Mayor, and His Longevity
2013-11-20.abstract: Among both supporters and detractors of Mayor Rob Ford, who has been stripped of most of his budget and staff, there seems to be agreement that he will not resign and could even win re-election.

2013-11-21.title: Money Traders Fret Over Possible U.S.-Iran Pact
2013-11-21.abstract: For most of the last decade, particularly since Western financial sanctions began to bite hard two years ago, the dollar has been king around Tehran’s currency bazaar.

2013-11-21.title: Robert Bowie, 104, Adviser to Truman, Eisenhower, Johnson and Carter, Dies
2013-11-21.abstract: Robert R. Bowie, a Harvard foreign policy expert, was an analyst on the Cold War, national security and conflicts around the globe.

2013-11-21.title: Condom Contest Produces 812 Ideas for Improvement
2013-11-21.abstract: The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation is seeking to decrease unintended pregnancies and sexually transmitted diseases with “a next-generation condom.”

2013-11-21.title: Raw Sewage and Anger Flood Gaza’s Streets as Electricity Runs Low
2013-11-21.abstract: A shortage of electricity and cheap diesel fuel from Egypt led the Hamas government to shut down Gaza’s lone power plant, which caused sewage stations to stop working.

2013-11-21.title: Aid Groups Get Strong Response to Philippines Appeals
2013-11-21.abstract: The United Nations has already raised $129 million, about half the amount it requested in an emergency appeal last week to provide disaster relief in the Philippines.

2013-11-22.title: As Syrian War Grinds On, a New Flock of Refugees Takes Flight
2013-11-22.abstract: Many Syrian men who have fled to Turkey, Lebanon or Jordan to escape the war have taken their passion for pigeon collecting with them.

2013-11-22.title: U.S. and China Find Convergence on Climate Issue
2013-11-22.abstract: With China having recently surpassed the United States as the world’s largest emitter of greenhouse gases, the countries are finding that their interests increasingly overlap, officials say.

2013-11-22.title: 2 Lions, Their Birth Hailed by Gaza, Die Within Days
2013-11-22.abstract: The two cubs, welcomed as a triumph over the hardships of occupation and a symbol of Palestinian resistance, lived only a few days.

2013-11-22.title: Font of Natural Energy in the Philippines, Crippled by Nature
2013-11-22.abstract: A superstorm consistent with some scientists’ warnings about climate change has done tremendous damage to an island that is one of the world’s biggest success stories of renewable energy.

2013-11-22.title: African Rebel Leader Said to Be Considering Surrender
2013-11-22.abstract: There are reports that Joseph Kony, who has been indicted by the International Criminal Court, might be ill and ready to turn himself in.

2013-11-23.title: Fighter of Corruption in Nigeria Considers Next Steps
2013-11-23.abstract: Nuhu Ribadu, who has been celebrated inside Nigeria and beyond for his five-year tenure as chief of an anticorruption unit, has not ruled out a bid for the presidency.

2013-11-23.title: Editor in India, Known for Investigations Into Corruption, Is Accused of Rape
2013-11-23.abstract: Rape and sexual assault charges have been filed against Tarun Tejpal, the editor of Tehelka, a liberal-minded magazine that has influenced a generation of young Indian journalists.

2013-11-23.title: Norwegian, 22, Takes World Chess Title
2013-11-23.abstract: Magnus Carlsen, a 22-year-old Norwegian, defeated Viswanathan Anand, 43, of India, the titleholder since 2007, and he did not lose a game in the best-of-12 series.

2013-11-23.title: Japan: Governor of Tokyo Embroiled in Loan Scandal
2013-11-23.abstract: The governor of Tokyo, Naoki Inose, received a half-million dollars from a hospital operator involved in an election-fraud case.

2013-11-23.title: A Gamble in Iran Talks: Easing of the Sanctions
2013-11-23.abstract: President Obama’s critics — in Congress, the Arab world and Israel — argue that he has the strategy backward in negotiating a freeze of Iran’s nuclear program.

2013-11-24.title: Women in Domestic Slavery Case Lived in a ‘Collective’ With Suspect, Police Say
2013-11-24.abstract: Two of three women who say they were held for 30 years in a London home had lived in a group with a suspect in the case, the police said.

2013-11-24.title: A Step, if Modest, Toward Slowing Iran’s Weapons Capability
2013-11-24.abstract: The interim deal brings the Obama administration a step closer to its ultimate goal: lengthening the time it takes Iran to build a weapon.

2013-11-24.title: Elders Back Security Pact That Karzai Won’t Sign
2013-11-24.abstract: Leaders who had gathered in Kabul asked President Hamid Karzai on Sunday to sign the agreement promptly, but Mr. Karzai said that he wanted to continue negotiations.

2013-11-24.title: Israeli Leaders Denounce Geneva Accord
2013-11-24.abstract: Officials said that they were not bound by the agreement on Iran’s nuclear program and that Israel would be ready to defend itself without assistance from allies.

2013-11-24.title: Thousands Protest Ukraine’s Rejection of Trade Pacts
2013-11-24.abstract: Tens of thousands of people marched in Kiev to protest the Ukrainian government’s decision to drop trade pacts with the European Union and instead focus on improving ties with Russia.

2013-11-25.title: Close Vote Raises Tensions in Honduras
2013-11-25.abstract: Hondurans went to the polls on Sunday to elect a new president at the end of a long campaign that has cracked open the ossified politics of one of Latin America’s poorest countries.

2013-11-25.title: Nuclear Accord With Iran Opens Diplomatic Doors in the Mideast
2013-11-25.abstract: The preliminary accord reached with Iran is a seminal moment for President Obama, but experts say seeking a broader diplomatic opening could alter other American calculations.

2013-11-25.title: 家居| 第五大道上的宫殿
2013-11-25.abstract: 室内设计师霍华德·斯拉特金让《纽约时报-国际生活》的萨拉·拉芬·科斯特洛参观了他在第五大道上宫殿似的公寓。

2013-11-25.title: Evacuation Questions After China Pipe Blast
2013-11-25.abstract: A deadly explosion in the city of Qingdao came seven hours after initial reports of a leak in a pipeline, prompting some news outlets to ask why a more thorough evacuation had not been carried out.

2013-11-25.title: The Bard of the Tea Stall
2013-11-25.abstract: Laxman Rao of Delhi offers something that most tea sellers, known as chai wallahs, do not – books that he has written and published himself.

2013-11-26.title: Iraq: Attacks Kill at Least 23 People
2013-11-26.abstract: A series of bombings and shootings on Monday left at least 23 people dead and more than 20 wounded, Iraqi security officials and medical workers said.

2013-11-26.title: The Vatican: Putin Meets the Pope
2013-11-26.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia met with Pope Francis in the Vatican on Monday, and their first encounter was primarily focused on the Middle East.

2013-11-26.title: Israelis See Ticking Clock, and Alternate Approaches, on Iran and Palestinians
2013-11-26.abstract: To some, Israel’s differing approach to the Iran nuclear negotiations and to peace talks with the Palestinians is a sign of hypocrisy. Others say it is an existential necessity.

2013-11-26.title: New Clues May Change Buddha’s Date of Birth
2013-11-26.abstract: At a temple in Nepal that contains a pillar documenting a homage at the Buddha’s birthplace, researchers determined that lower structures were erected as early as the sixth century B.C.

2013-11-26.title: Egypt’s Government Struggles to Gain Footing as Dissent Grows
2013-11-26.abstract: It has become harder for officials to blame the Muslim Brotherhood for all the nation’s problems, nearly five months after it was swept from power.

2013-11-27.title: Ras Marcus
2013-11-27.abstract: Ras Marcus, an award-winning Calypsonian artist, singing “Go Fuh Cup.”

2013-11-27.title: Bassem Youssef on Egypt’s Polarization
2013-11-27.abstract: For Mr. Youssef, a popular Egyptian satirist whose television show was recently suspended, Egypt today is more polarized than ever.

2013-11-27.title: Karzai’s Bet: U.S. Is Bluffing in Warning on Security Pact
2013-11-27.abstract: Even many of President Hamid Karzai’s friends have criticized his refusal to conclude a security agreement between Afghanistan and the United States.

2013-11-27.title: Iraq: Suicide Bombers Kill 16 Soldiers and Policemen
2013-11-27.abstract: Four suicide bombers struck northern Baghdad, targeting an army base and a police station, killing 10 soldiers and six policemen, police and medical officials said.

2013-11-27.title: Egyptian Riot Police Attack Peaceful Protests in Cairo
2013-11-27.abstract: Officers beat, sexually harassed and detained some of Egypt’s most prominent human rights activists in a crackdown that could broaden opposition to the military-backed government.

2013-11-28.title: Not for Prime Time: Music Video With Iran’s President
2013-11-28.abstract: A music video honoring President Hassan Rouhani includes features banned on state TV.

2013-11-28.title: Deadly Accident at Stadium Raises Worry on Brazil’s World Cup Aims
2013-11-28.abstract: A construction crane collapse at the site of the São Paulo stadium where the 2014 World Cup is scheduled to open puts a spotlight on increasing tensions among Brazilians about cost overruns, delays and the country’s priorities.

2013-11-28.title: China’s Move Puts Airspace in Spotlight
2013-11-28.abstract: Who has the right to fly where is an issue that has created confusion, and danger, in places affected by territorial disputes.

2013-11-28.title: Mystery of Missing Mushrooms Leaves French Blaming Roma
2013-11-28.abstract: Competition between established French companies and outside mushroom pickers has brought with it darker undercurrents of hostility and discrimination.

2013-11-28.title: Egypt: 14 Female Protesters Receive 11-Year Sentences
2013-11-28.abstract: In addition, seven girls ranging from 15 to 17 years old were also sentenced to an indeterminate term at a juvenile facility for joining the protest.

2013-11-29.title: Russia: Court Grants Bail to Last Detainee in Greenpeace Case
2013-11-29.abstract: A court in St. Petersburg granted bail on Thursday to Colin Russell, the last of the 30 people on a Greenpeace International ship seized in September after a protest in the Arctic.

2013-11-29.title: Syrian Forces Press Rebels With Gains
2013-11-29.abstract: With fighters from Hezbollah, which is based in Lebanon, Syrian government troops gained control of the town of Deir Attiyah, which straddles a critical highway.

2013-11-29.title: Disillusionment Grows Among Syrian Opposition as Fighting Drags On
2013-11-29.abstract: More people are turning their backs on the revolt and becoming disaffected by the growing power of Islamists and the disarray, division and greed among rebel commanders.

2013-11-29.title: Hard Times for a Small (and Fuzzy) Group of Europeans
2013-11-29.abstract: In an era of austerity, donkeys have been swept up in the debate over just how far the European Union should go to maintain its farming regions.

2013-11-29.title: Ire in Canada Over Report N.S.A. Spied From Ottawa
2013-11-29.abstract: Documents leaked by Edward J. Snowden describe surveillance during a summit meeting of world leaders in Canada in June 2010.

2013-11-30.title: Chemical Weapons Watchdog Rejects a Request by Syria to Convert Arms Sites
2013-11-30.abstract: The group turned down a proposal for 12 of the facilities to be used for storage.

2013-11-30.title: A Forced Adoption, a Lifetime Quest and a Longing That Never Waned
2013-11-30.abstract: In a home for unwed mothers in 1950s Ireland, Philomena Lee’s son was forcibly removed from her. Now, as Judi Dench portrays her in the film version of her story, Ms. Lee speaks of her search and its lessons.

2013-11-30.title: Mideast Tensions Alarm U.N. Chief
2013-11-30.abstract: Ban Ki-moon, the United Nations secretary general, expressed alarm about what he called the “increasingly dangerous situation on the ground” in the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.

2013-11-30.title: North Korea Accuses Captive U.S. Veteran of War Crimes
2013-11-30.abstract: The country’s official news agency says Merrill E. Newman, 85, who was detained in October while visiting as a tourist, has acknowledged killing civilians during the Korean War.

2013-11-30.title: Part II: Adam Minter on China’s Scrap Trade and the Environment
2013-11-30.abstract: The journalist Adam Minter examined how China became the world’s largest importer of scrap metal and describes how this has environmental benefits.
